title: Geotechnical Engineer
description: **Dublin or Cork \| Ireland** **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Ireland.** ***Engineer the ground beneath tomorrow’s most critical infrastructure.*** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach have set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Ground Engineering team is the largest in Ireland, bringing together geotechnical engineers, hydrogeologists, contaminated land specialists, and engineering geologists. We deliver innovative projects with creativity and passion, shaping the built environment across Ireland and the wider European business. **The Opportunity** This is an excellent opportunity to join a highly specialised team and contribute to a dynamic portfolio of complex, multidisciplinary projects. You will play a key role in shaping some of the most significant infrastructure and development schemes in Ireland and across Europe. While the role focuses on geotechnical engineering design and delivery, you will also gain valuable exposure to the full breadth of Ground Engineering disciplines. This position offers a stimulating and supportive environment to advance your technical, business, and people skills, working alongside industry experts and developing relationships with our firm’s wider engineering community. It also offers the opportunity to place sustainability at the heart of engineering solutions. **As a Geotechnical Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Deliver geotechnical design and analysis for foundations, retaining structures, slope stability, and earthworks on major international commissions (e.g., hyperscale data centres in Europe, the Fehmarnsund Link) and nationally significant Irish infrastructure (e.g., Metrolink, DART\+, N6 Galway Ring Road). * Prepare high\-quality design reports, technical specifications, desk studies, and site walkovers for educational, pharmaceutical, industrial, and commercial developments. * Plan, supervise, and interpret geotechnical ground investigations, providing technical support during the construction phase, including periodic site visits. * Incorporate the latest digital tools, automation, and numerical analysis into our projects to enhance project delivery. * Develop your project management and commercial awareness skills, while supporting and mentoring junior colleagues as you strengthen your own leadership capabilities. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for a motivated and ambitious engineer looking to build a strong foundation in geotechnical design. We also value: * An honours Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Geotechnical, or Structural Engineering (or equivalent). A post\-graduate qualification in Geotechnical Engineering or Soil Mechanics is an advantage. * Over 3 years of post\-graduate experience in geotechnical design and/or construction. * Strong technical understanding of the design of deep excavations, deep and shallow foundations, earthworks, slope stability, and soft ground solutions. * Good planning, organisational, and time management skills, with the ability to maintain personal effectiveness across several projects and communicate confidently in both technical and non\-technical settings. * Experience in planning, managing, supervising, and interpreting ground investigation contracts, and/or experience in numerical analysis and GIS. * A commitment to continuous professional development, ideally working towards Chartered status, with the flexibility to travel and work on\-site (a full driver’s licence is a strong advantage). **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth and Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being and Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** Apply here https://www.arup.com/careers/

title: Global CLO Trade Administrator
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **Title / Grade: Global CLO Trade Administrator** **Business:** Global Corporate Trust – CLO Deal Administration **Location:** Dublin (Hyrbid) **Reports to:** Global CLO Trade Administrator– Team Manager **Position Responsibilities** The Collateralized Loan Obligation (CLO) Group is a division within Global Corporate Trust (GCT) division. CLOs are structured finance vehicles that issue both loan and equity liabilities, with the proceeds being used to buy a portfolio of collateral. Typical types of collateral include syndicated bank loans, corporate bonds \& equity. We are establishing a centralized Global Trades Administration Team to enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and timeliness of trade processing across our CLO platform. This team will serve as a Center of Excellence, assuming full ownership of the end\-to\-end pending trade lifecycle, driving improvements in Straight Through Processing (STP) rates and reconciliation accuracy. **Context / Environment for this role** **Key working relationships** The role holder will be responsible for cultivating strong working relationships across the CLO Group, collaborating within a specialist team dedicated to pending trade operations. Success in this role requires a commitment to teamwork, meticulous attention to detail, and the ability to consistently meet tight deadlines. The ideal candidate will demonstrate deep technical expertise and the capacity to manage a demanding and diverse workload, ensuring the highest standards of accuracy and efficiency in trade processing **Key Responsibilities:** **Trade Processing \& Oversight** * Review and process pending trades received via STP, user driven trade loaders and manual trade inputs. * Investigate and resolve STP failures, liaising with internal stakeholders and clients as needed. * Manage trade intake templates and ensure accurate trade data ingestion into CDO Suite **Client \& Stakeholder Engagement** * Provide feedback to clients on trade output formatting and data quality issues. * Collaborate with CLO Deal Admin, Analytics, and Client Management teams to streamline trade workflows. * Support client onboarding and transitions to STP processes. **Reconciliation \& Reporting** * Monitor and reconcile pending trades using end user computing (EUC) tools and applications. * Maintain accurate records of trade statuses and contribute to daily cash file accuracy. * Prepare and distribute reports on STP failures, failed trades, and break resolution metrics. **Process Improvement \& Governance** * Participate in UAT testing for system enhancements related to pending trades. * Contribute to the development of SOPs, SLAs, and internal controls. * Support audit and compliance reviews by maintaining robust documentation and audit trails. **Required Skills \& Experience:** * 2–5 years of experience in trade operations, loan administration, or financial services. * Familiarity with CLO structures, bank loans, and bond trade processing. * Experience with portfolio administration platforms such as CDO Suite. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, particularly in exception management. * Excellent communication skills and stakeholder management experience. * Proficiency in Excel and exposure to workflow tools or dashboards is advantageous. **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: Job Description: Are you an enthusiastic **Graduate Electrical Engineer**? IPS is hiring! Join our industry\-leading team at our European office based in **Dublin, Ireland** and become part of our enriching Graduate Programme. The Graduate Electrical Engineer will undertake specific tasks on project teams using their education, creativity and expertise to resolve engineering problems and tasks as they are assigned. A pool of knowledge and experience from all of the IPS teams across the world will be available to our graduates and, consequently, more experienced staff will always be available when a graduate engineer needs support. Job Duties and Responsibilities* As a graduate Electrical Engineer, you will get the opportunity to work on world class projects for global clients within the Life Sciences sector. * Work with a professional team of top industry experts in the delivery of complex facilities * Participate in engineering design reviews (GMP, safety, operational, maintenance) * Use computer assisted engineering and design software and equipment to prepare engineering and design documents * Research design options and documents findings for Senior Engineer and Line Manager * Research code issues and ask general questions of code officials * Ensure that drawings and designs are in accordance with IPS engineering and design standards * Promote and apply Health and Safety in all aspects of Electrical Design and Construction * Support design computations and quantity estimates for the smaller, less complex projects within the business unit (low voltage systems, fire alarm systems, backup power generation, electrical power distribution, life safety systems, etc.) * Support with calculations using engineering formulas and skills for design such as lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, etc. * Keep Project Manager informed of workload and any changes in project scope or additional services * Interface with engineers in our global network of offices * Support selecting equipment based on recommendations of Project Designer/Engineer and fills out equipment schedules. * Organise and maintain project files. Maintains a neat and organised work area which allows efficient access to information required by other team members. * Have a can\-do attitude and takes ownership of projects under the supervision of a Senior Electrical Engineer. Qualifications \& Requirements: * Level 8 Hons Degree in Electrical Services Engineering or Electrical Engineering (preferably a Masters degree). * Proven skills in computer operation for engineering design programs, Revit and computer aided drafting. * Some previous engineering/design experience would be desirable. * Desire to work towards achieving CEng with Engineers Ireland/CIBSE/I Mech E. * Desire to contribute to the development of Electrical Engineering Team within IPS * Good interpersonal skills and demonstrates the ability to work within multi discipline team All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with video required. About Us: IPS, a Berkshire Hathaway Company, is a global leader in developing innovative business solutions for the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industries. Through operational expertise and industry\-leading knowledge, skill, and passion, IPS provides consultancy services, architecture, engineering, project controls, construction management, and compliance services that allow clients to develop and manufacture life\-impacting products. Its newest acquisition, Linesight, specializes in cost, schedule, risk, program, and project management services in various market sectors, including data centers, life sciences, and high\-tech industries. With the addition of Linesight, IPS has over 3,000 professionals in over 45 offices across 17 countries in the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, Southeast Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV and Staffing Services. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race; creed; color; religion; national origin; sex; age; disability; sexual orientation; gender identity or expression; genetic predisposition or carrier status; veteran, marital, or citizenship status; or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer. *Attention Search Firms / Third\-Party Recruiters: IPS is not seeking assistance or accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for employment opportunities with IPS. Regardless of past practice, all resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at IPS via email, the Internet, or directly to hiring managers at IPS in any form without a valid written search agreement in place for a specific position will be deemed the sole property of IPS, and no fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by IPS as a result of the referral or through other means.*

description: Fingleton White provide multidiscipline engineering services for the energy industry. We provide a full range of design, construction and commissioning services and are active across many sectors including Renewables, Gas Networks, Water, Energy \& Utilities, and Asset Management. The company is responsible for planning and development of some of the most significant projects in the energy sector in Ireland. We have 8 offices across Ireland, Northern Ireland and England with over 200 staff. **Who We Are….** We have a proven track record of delivering lasting solutions which we design based on a first principles approach. Our priority is to get the engineering fundamentals right. Fingleton White is a company that has been built on sustainability. Our very first project was a hydroelectric power station. Over our 40\-year history, we have developed our own projects which have delivered CO emissions savings of well over 2 million tonnes. We are focussed on reducing our carbon footprint and having a positive impact on our environment and communities through our biodiversity initiatives, our energy efficiency programmes and implementing sustainable development targets. **Our Projects….** Achieving zero carbon will require a diverse range of solutions and approaches. Our clients require individualised pathways to ensure they can achieve CO reductions in a cost\-effective manner that reflects their specific business needs and goals. Fingleton White is a key solution provider in this complex journey. We are delivering renewables projects, carbon reduction projects, energy efficiency upgrades and improvements for our clients. We understand that natural gas, biomethane and hydrogen all have a key role in transitioning to low carbon and achieving zero carbon. Greening of gas networks will be fundamental for supporting intermittent renewable energy sources. Our Gas Networks design team are delivering projects in areas such as Biogas from Anaerobic Digestion, Carbon Capture, Hydrogen and Biomethane for grid injection and transport. Expertise in these areas will be key for the future as they provide unique and complex challenges that requires highly technical and specialised engineering solutions. **Our People….** Our people are our only asset and are our unique selling point. Our people are key to everything we do. Business and client requirements are constantly changing, it is essential that we provide the tools to allow our team to develop their skills and expertise to match these demands. We challenge our engineers to develop and progress within the organisation. Our Development and Progression Framework details how people can develop within their roles and progress their career within the organisation. Fingleton White is an Engineers Ireland's Continuing Professional Development (CPD) Accredited Employer and we have systems in place to provide employees with targeted training and experience relevant to their grade. Chartership is supported and encouraged through CPD and mentoring and is a requirement for senior engineering roles in the organisation. **Graduate Programme….** We will provide you with all the training you need, exposure to projects, responsibility and real engineering experience to maximise your talents as an engineer. Our 2\-year Graduate Training programme includes Graduate workshops where key engineering topics and other relevant professional working skills are taught to our new engineers. We also provide site visits and, on the job learning with coaching and management from senior personnel and peer review of designs. Our Graduate Programme is Engineering centric, and your focus will be on delivering engineering tasks. We do not provide a multi\-departmental graduate programme. Our goal is to develop engineering excellence. Position: **The Role….** The successful candidate will join an established team with senior support in one of our sectors. The Graduate Engineer will have the opportunity as their career progresses to acquire a broad range of skills and experience and to work across different areas of the business. The role will provide you with essential engineering, communications and project delivery skills which are transferrable across any engineering project. The following are examples of some of the types of project work that the Graduate Engineer will have the opportunity to work on: * Design from conceptual phase through to project completion * Development of Preliminary and Detailed Design reports, calculations and drawings * Design \& Project coordination from conceptual phase through to project completion in the Gas network sector in areas such as: * Transmission AGIs, Pipelines and Diversions * Distribution Installations, Services and Pipelines * Renewable Gas Projects including Biogas, Biomethane and Hydrogen Power Generation Station \& Balance of Plant Design * Design \& implementation of energy audits and energy saving projects * Design of Biomass Combined Heat \& Power projects from conceptual phase through to project completion * Renewable Energy feasibility studies and projects including solar, biogas, hydro and others * Industrial utilities design, HVAC, steam, refrigeration, compressed air * Design of water infrastructure The Graduate Design Engineer will have the opportunity to progress their career within Fingleton White to be involved in the various business sectors to provide experience across a wide range of projects. The location for this role will be our Dublin office, however we will also consider Portlaoise, Cork, Galway, Kilkenny, Antrim, Hemel Hempstead or Birmingham. Requirements: **The Candidate….** The ideal candidate will have: * Engineering degree (Hons Bachelor degree or higher) in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, Energy, Electrical or a similar discipline relevant to the specification above. * 0 \- 2 years post\-graduate experience. * Excellent computer skills with proficiency in Microsoft Office. * Good academic record * Full clean driving license and own transport required * Fluent written and spoken English is essential * Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to work on own initiative or as part of a team Other information: **What We Offer….** * An attractive remuneration package. * Paid overtime * Career progression in line with published plan and educational support * Year on year salary reviews * Paid Paternity \& Maternity Leave * Continuous Professional Development \& Pathway to Chartership. * Life Assurance * Employee Assistance Programme * Hybrid Working (remote and office) \& Flexible Working Policy * Group pension scheme with employer contribution * Professional Certifications, pathways to become a Chartered Engineer * Employee wellness programmes * Participation in ESG activities such as sustainability initiatives, biodiversity committee, STEPS to Engineering, Women in Engineering initiative * Employee social outings

title: HR Service Partners - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank is seeking to recruit for its Human Resources Directorate (HR) – HR Business Partnering Division (HRBP) \- HR Service Partner Unit (HRSP), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, **HR Service Partners\*** . * Benchmark role Senior/Principal Operational Assistant. ***These are full time temporary positions at grade 2/3\.*** **The Terms Of These Contracts Will Be** ***Until 31/12/2027 for the first position*** ***Until 31/12/2028 for the second position*** ***Panel interviews are anticipated for July/August 2026*** Purpose Provide operational and administrative support to HR Business Partnering activities in order to deliver an optimal HR service to managers and colleagues across the Bank, ensuring the right level of data confidentiality. Operating Network The HR Service Partner reports to the Head of HR Service Partners unit and work closely with one or more HR Business Partners in supporting the managers and teams in the client directorates on general HR matters. He/She interfaces with other HR services and with managers and staff members of the client directorates. The post requires flexibility, autonomy and the ability to prioritise and handle several tasks simultaneously, while exercising a high attention to detail. It also requires a high level of discretion due to the confidential nature of the matters under the remit of HR Department. Accountabilities * Support in delivering consistent and high\-quality HR processes with the objective of ensuring an efficient functioning of the HRBP activities, focusing on amongst others: + Performance and talent review cycles + Staff transfers and temporary assignments + Contract extensions and conversions + Reorganisations + HR Reporting to managers * Manage and maintain HR data (e.g. Peoplesoft/Oracle Cloud HCM , ensuring accuracy and confidentiality. * Perform regular data checks and contribute to reliable reporting for decision\-making. * Provide analysis of available HR reports for the designated Directorates to the HRBPs or directly to managers to support the decision\-making process on HR matters, providing feedback to the relevant teams when adjustments or corrections are needed. * Execute other regular or ad\-hoc HR operational and administrative tasks for the covered Directorate(s) in alignment with the respective HRBP(s). * Initiate improvements and participate in the optimisation of working methods, processes and technologies and raise new ideas that ultimately foster customer satisfaction and efficiency. * When needed, ensure integration and on\-the\-job training of more junior colleagues. * Maintain permanent relationships and good cooperation with Expert HR teams (Talent Acquisition, Performance, Learning \& Development, Talent Management, Customer Service, etc.) to guarantee an optimal flow of information within HR. * Build and maintain good working relationships with the clients in the business, peers and internal counterparts. **Qualifications** * Secondary level education, complemented with a 2\-year certification in a relevant field or secondary level education with equally qualified experience. * At least 5 years of experience in an administrative support role or operational position, with at least 2 years of experience in HR administration with a solid knowledge of HR processes. * Strong collaboration and organinsation skills, pro\-active attitude, solution and service oriented. * Developed sense of discretion. * Ability to multi\-task, prioritize, and manage time effectively. * Good knowledge of the standard office tools and particularly advanced skills in Excel. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage). Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **19th June 2026**

title: Hiring for 2026! Entry-Level English Teacher (ESL) | No Experience Needed
description: **Launch Your International Career with Purpose and Adventure** Are you searching for a **rewarding, entry\-level job** that lets you **travel the world** , **work abroad** , and gain **real\-world experience** all while making a difference? Whether you're a **recent graduate** , exploring a **career change** , or an **ESL/TEFL\-certified teacher** ready to take your skills overseas, this is the opportunity you’ve been looking for. **English 1** is now hiring **motivated, globally minded individuals** for **full\-time English teaching jobs in China** . No teaching experience? No problem. We provide **full training** , **ready\-made teaching materials** , and **visa sponsorship,** making this the perfect **graduate job** , **first job** , or **international career move** for anyone ready to start something new. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** . title: IT Airport Engineer - Dublin Airport
description: **Brief Description** **IT Airport Engineer \- Dublin Airport** Working as an Airport Engineer, you’ll provide and maintain continuity of service for all components of the Airport passenger processing system and resolve any technical issues, including more complex issues requiring specialist knowledge, which may occur. **Key Accountabilities** * Carry out regular routine and preventative maintenance on the range of passenger processing equipment within the airport. * Respond to all customer incidents and service requests passed across by the Global Service Desk and resolve any user faults within agreed SLAs * Troubleshoot and resolve all airline host and connectivity issues * Maintain a high level of communication at all time with both colleagues and senior members of staff and provide timely updates to the Global Service Desk so that progress against each individual incident can be updated as required. * Resolve any issues escalated by the Airport Technicians in a timely manner to ensure that customer SLAs can still be met. * Offer support and on\-site training and coaching to the Airport SSK engineer to increase their understanding and skill in resolving incidents. * Identify any spares required and advise the Global Service Desk accordingly to ensure that the customer incident can be updated accordingly. * Carry out regular "floor\-walking" and pro\-active line of sight checks to identify and resolve any issues before they are raised as incidents through the Global Service Desk. **Requirements** **Skills and Qualifications:** * Good knowledge and experience in Microsoft Desktop software is a requirement. * An understanding of network protocols, server hardware and configurations or other IT qualifications * CompTIA A\+ Desirable * Self\-motivated with a willingness to learn and adapt to any new change or situation * Good verbal and written communication skills * Must be open to work on a Fixed term contract **About Us** ESP Global Services is a leader in providing customized, 24/7 IT support solutions to the aviation industry. With a dedicated service delivery team, we provide seamless day\-to\-day support via on\-site engineers, responsive visiting engineers, and a multilingual service desk that manages over 1,000 tickets per day for more than 200 customers worldwide. **Why Join Us?** * Work in a critical and fast\-paced environment with opportunities to make an immediate impact on the operations of a major international airport. * Be part of a supportive and collaborative team, committed to professional development and innovation. * Receive competitive compensation, including shift allowances and a comprehensive benefits package. * Career growth opportunities within a leading IT support provider in the aviation sector. Join a team that values excellence – our ISO 9001 certification reflects our commitment to quality and superior customer service.

title: IT Helpdesk Engineer
company: MCS Group | Your Specialist Recruitment Consultancy
description: **Job Title:** IT Support Engineer **Location:** Dublin (On\-site) **Salary:** Up to €45,000 \+ bonus \+ many other amazing benefits About the Company We're working with a long\-standing Dublin\-based business that has built a strong reputation over the years and continues to grow. They're a team of 250\+ people, working on large, high\-profile projects with some really well\-known global clients. It's a busy, fast\-moving environment, but one that genuinely puts a lot of emphasis on culture, collaboration, and keeping things enjoyable day to day. From a tech perspective, they've invested well and continue to evolve, so you're not stepping into something stale or overly rigid. The Opportunity This is the kind of IT Support role people often say they want, but don't always find. Yes, there are tickets. But this isn't a "sit behind a screen and close tickets all day" type job. You'll be out on the floor, speaking to people, solving problems in real time, and becoming a go\-to person across the business. The role naturally gives you a lot of visibility, and that's something the team really values. You'll be joining a small IT team where everyone gets stuck in. There's a good mix of day\-to\-day support and exposure to wider systems and projects, so you're constantly learning rather than just repeating the same tasks. It's a great move for someone who feels like they've outgrown a standard service desk role and wants something a bit more hands\-on and engaging. What You'll Be Doing You'll be supporting the wider business with whatever comes up day to day \- from user issues and device setups to getting involved in broader fixes and improvements. Some days will be busier than others (roughly 15\-30 tickets between the team), but the key difference here is how the team works together. It's collaborative, you're not left on your own, and there's a shared responsibility to get things sorted quickly. A big part of the role is also how you interact with people. You'll be dealing with everyone from junior staff to senior leaders, so being able to explain things clearly and build trust is just as important as your technical ability. What They're Looking For * Around 3\-5 years' experience in IT Support * Someone comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment * Solid fundamentals across Microsoft technologies and general troubleshooting * Most importantly, someone who enjoys working with people and being visible in the business They're not overly focused on certifications. If you want to pursue them, they'll support you, but attitude and approach matter more here. How This Role Works This is an on\-site role, and that's intentional. Because of how hands\-on and people\-facing the role is, being in the office is a big part of it. There is flexibility to work from home one day a week once you've settled in, but day\-to\-day you'll be in the mix with the wider team. What's on Offer Salary is up to €45,000, alongside a solid benefits package including bonus, pension, learning support, and a genuinely social working environment. It's the kind of place where people know each other, get involved, and enjoy coming into work \- which makes a big difference in a role like this. If you're in that 3\-5 year range and want a role where you're learning, visible, and actually part of the business rather than just supporting it from the sidelines, this is well worth a conversation. How to Apply To apply for this role, submit your CV via the application form provided. Alternatively, to speak in absolute confidence about this opportunity, please contact Jack Tyrrell via j.tyrrell@mcsgroup.jobs Disclaimer If you have a disability which means you require assistance at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us directly to discuss. We are committed to providing equality of opportunity to all. Please note, we are receiving an exceptionally high number of applications at present and will be unable to shortlist candidates who are not meeting the specific requirements for this role. Due to the high volume of applications, we may be unable to provide individual feedback. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

title: IT Program Project Delivery, Vice President

establish clear responsibilities with various partner teams * Ambitious and proactive: able to show initiative and come up with ideas without prompting * Ability to think on your feet: be able to suggest solutions as issues come to light * Demonstrated sense of curiosity and the courage to ask tough questions * A quantitative approach to problem solving and a collaborative implementer to holistic solutions; a systems thinker * Deep focus on execution, follow\-through, accountability, and results * Exceptional cross\-team collaboration; able to work across different functions, organizations, and reporting boundaries to get the job done. **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * 10\+ years of experience in financial services or a related industry, including experience engaging with leadership executives and successfully driving and delivering complex and impactful strategic change programs * You have experience in technical program management in a dynamic and highly technical environment, a strong track record in managing complex cross\-functional projects. Technical aptitude, understanding technology in a business context * Solid knowledge and understanding of investment management products and asset types, including but not limited to ETFs, mutual funds, fixed income, equities, derivatives, and alternatives * Experience in business process analysis, design, and implementation * Experience navigating a complex matrix of accountable stakeholders and global teams in multiple time zones * Ability to manage multiple complex projects at the same time * Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication * Comprehensive knowledge of design metrics, analytics tools, benchmarking activities and related reporting to identify best practices * Demonstrated analytic/diagnostic skills * Ability to work in a matrix environment and partner with virtual teams * Ability to work independently, multi\-task, and take ownership of various parts of a project or initiative * Ability to negotiate, be objective, to prioritize, and to give a balanced view * Ability to work under pressure and manage to tight deadlines or unexpected changes in expectations or requirements * Positive, energetic, and enthusiastic approach **About State Street** ====================== Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. title: IT Support Analyst

title: Installation Technician III

Our highly certified industry experts and innovation leaders share a vision to help people work smarter and live better. We believe that success starts with a sound team and that an inclusive and diverse workplace moves us all forward. **WHAT YOU’LL DO** The Installation Technician 3 will perform installation tasks for AVI\-SPL contracted projects including but not limited to: cable pulls, cable terminations, system equipment and components, rack fabrication and wiring layout for equipment and metal fabrication. Work hours may vary based on client requirements and may include travel to various locations in support of the account. * This position will generally work on customer sites and may deal with a construction site type of work environment. **WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR** **Must\-Haves:** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present technical information to both technical and non\-technical audiences. work. * Advanced understanding of audio\-visual systems, including projectors, screens, speakers, microphones, video conferencing equipment, digital signage, and control systems. * Advanced proficiency in installing, terminating, and troubleshooting low\-voltage cabling (e.g., HDMI, CAT5e/6, fiber optic), and experience with conduit runs, cable trays, and other wiring methods. * Advanced ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues with audiovisual equipment and systems. * Advanced understanding of network infrastructure and how it integrates with audiovisual systems (e.g., IP\-based systems, streaming, cloud\-based services). * Advanced ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues with audiovisual equipment and systems. * Ability to use hand and power tools in a safe and efficient manner. * Ability to work and think independently to meet deadlines. * Ability to lead and mentor junior technicians and manage site teams on large complex projects. * Advanced knowledge and use of specific test equipment for required applications. * Advanced knowledge and application of industry installation safety codes and standards. * Routinely advises Project Manager on installation related concepts affecting project planning, scheduling, and budgeting. * Routinely advises Project Engineer in developing solutions to complex technical issues. * Routinely assists Programmer in loading and testing code on site. * External Training: * Valid and current AVIXA CTS General Certification required. * Valid Driver’s License and a Motor Vehicle Record that meets AVI\-SPL Driving Privileges standards. * Minimum of 4 \- 6 years in audiovisual, electronics or related industry installation experience required . * Minimum of a High School Diploma or equivalent title: Investment Advisor
description: A well\-established international investment management firm is continuing to grow its private client business in Ireland and is seeking to hire an Investment Advisor to join its Dublin\-based team. This is a client\-facing role focused on managing and developing long\-term relationships with high\-net\-worth individuals, providing ongoing guidance, support and education around investment portfolios and market developments. **The Opportunity** You will manage a portfolio of high\-net\-worth clients, acting as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor. Working closely with internal investment and research teams, you will be responsible for: * Building and maintaining strong, long\-term client relationships * Communicating portfolio performance, investment strategy and market updates * Explaining asset allocation decisions and economic developments in a clear and accessible way * Supporting clients through different market cycles and maintaining confidence in long\-term strategies * Acting as a conduit between clients and internal teams, ensuring a high standard of service delivery Investment decisions are made centrally by a dedicated investment team, allowing you to focus on client engagement and relationship management. **What We’re Looking For** We are keen to speak with individuals who have experience working with private clients in a financial services environment. **Typical backgrounds may include:** * Wealth management / private client advisory * Financial planning * Stockbroking * Private banking **You will ideally have:** * Experience dealing directly with high\-net\-worth individuals * A strong understanding of financial markets and investment principles * The ability to communicate complex investment concepts clearly * A relationship\-focused approach with a high level of professionalism * A genuine interest in markets and client outcomes **QFA qualification (or working towards it) is required.** **Working Environment** * Flexible working model * Strong emphasis on work\-life balance and autonomy * Collaborative team environment with ongoing training and development * Clear progression opportunities based on individual strengths and interests **Compensation** * Competitive base salary * Performance\-related bonus linked to client engagement and retention * Comprehensive benefits package **Why Consider This Role?** This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a long\-term career in investment advisory within a stable, client\-focused environment. The role offers the chance to work closely with high\-net\-worth clients, deepen your investment expertise, and develop as a trusted advisor without the pressure of a traditional sales\-driven role. If you’d like to explore this opportunity in confidence, please get in touch directly.

title: Investment Corporate Actions Representative III/Associate Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Corporate Actions Analyst – Income \& Class Actions.** **Location: Dublin** Perform daily tasks associated with the processing and reconciliation of voluntary and mandatory corporate actions, as well as research Class Action cases. Process corporate actions promptly and correctly with policies, procedures, and regulations. Interact with various groups across the organization as well as external vendors and business partners. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience. * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable. * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience. The **Purpose** of Your Role * Conduct and keep track of corporate action activities. * Conduct processing of all types of corporate action events, both voluntary and mandatory. * Facilitate the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions and provide daily cash estimates. * Coordinate corporate action executions, ensure the collection of income to the funds, resolve position differences, and facilitate securities lending activities. * Assist the team with corporate action research and process tasks related to assets under management. * Collaborate with stakeholders on issues and continuously follow up to resolve them. * Escalate high\-risk items or seek assistance to mitigate loss. * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Display a strong sense of teamwork. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality processing of Mandatory and Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Company Overview** At Fidelity, we are focused on making our financial expertise broadly accessible and effective in helping people live the lives they want. title: Junior Process Engineer - Fuze Services
description: At Fuze Health, we put patients first and tirelessly address the most pressing needs in healthcare. We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs. Fuze Health’s foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology\-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. **Junior Process Engineer** We are looking for a Jr.Process Engineer for our Manufacturing facility in Northern Cross. You will report to the Senior Process Engineer and become involved in the reactive and planned maintenance and development of facilities equipment, layout and infrastructure. Provide support to the project for Facilities, Process and Validation. We are looking for a dynamic and hands\-on Junior Process Engineer for our state\-of\-the\-art manufacturing facility in Northern Cross, Dublin. Reporting to the Senior Process Engineer, this role offers a unique, fast\-paced opportunity to work across both Cleanroom environments and our continuous flowline production for our personal home diagnostic kits. As a Junior Process Engineer, you will bridge the gap between process optimization and mechanical upkeep. You will be responsible for driving efficiency through Lean methodologies, keeping technical documentation flawless, and working directly on the floor to support our Maintenance team with equipment troubleshooting and reliability. **Responsibilities Include, But Are Not Limited To, The Following** * Duties will be variable depending on the nature of the production needs and ongoing engineering projects. Core responsibilities include: * Process Optimization \& Lean: Support the implementation of Lean manufacturing methods to eliminate waste, improve cycle times, and optimize continuous flowline efficiency. * Work Instructions \& Documentation: Create, review, and update standard operating procedures (SOPs) and Work Instructions (WIs) to ensure operational excellence and compliance. * Time \& Motion Analysis: Assist in conducting time analyses, capacity studies, and line balancing to maximize production throughput. * Maintenance \& Operations Support: Provide hands\-on support to the Maintenance team for planned (preventative) and reactive maintenance, calibration, and troubleshooting of plant and automated equipment. * Cleanroom \& Flowline Support: Ensure optimal equipment performance, workstation layout, and general infrastructure across both Cleanroom and standard manufacturing areas. * Validation Assistance: Support the Process Engineering and Validation teams in executing equipment test protocols (IQ/OQ/PQ) and compiling technical reports. * Automation \& Robotics: Assist in the basic diagnostics and maintenance of our packaging and automation systems. * Health \& Safety: Maintain compliance with safety standards and participate in H\&S programs related to facility and process upgrades. **What are we looking for?** * Education: 3rd level qualification in Engineering (Manufacturing, Mechanical, Biomedical, Process, or equivalent). * Experience: 1\+ years of experience in a manufacturing environment (internship/placement experience considered), preferably within the medical device (ISO 13485\) or pharmaceutical industries. * Adaptability: Comfort working in a cleanroom environment and a willingness to be hands\-on with mechanical/maintenance tasks when needed. * Skills: Strong analytical mindset with a basic understanding of Lean Manufacturing concepts and time\-analysis tools. * Tech Savvy: Competency with documentation tools (e.g., Google Docs, MS Word) and ability to read technical diagrams/drawings. **Nice\-to\-Haves** * Previous exposure to automated packaging lines or automation systems. * Basic understanding of electrical wiring or circuit diagrams. **Benefits** Alongside an hourly rate of **€15\.86 \- €16\.40** we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension matching contribution * Annual Compensation Reviews * LetsGetChecked has a Flexible Annual Leave policy. * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company. * Bike to work scheme \- and secure bike storage onsite. * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave. At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Fuze Corporate Inc. and its subsidiaries respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. Please read our Candidate Privacy Notice which explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information about job applicants during the recruitment and hiring process.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Job Description: At IPS, we are global leaders in developing innovative solutions for the consulting, architecture, engineering, project controls, procurement, construction management, and CQV of technically complex facilities, primarily in the life sciences industry. We are looking for a talented **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our growing construction team to be based at our EMEA office located in **Dublin, Ireland**. We are looking to appoint a construction industry professional with expert knowledge of construction costs and contracts. In this role, you will be responsible for managing all aspects of the contractual and financial side of construction projects and ensuring that construction projects are completed within their projected budget. The Quantity Surveyor assists with the valuation of construction works, bidding and project budgeting, and the submission of bills to the client, in addition to estimating services to projects and business development efforts. Job Duties and Responsibilities* Prepares service estimates for projects as required to support the achievement of corporate objectives * Prepares constructed cost estimates for projects as required to support achievement of corporate objectives * Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of estimating programs and systems for the corporation * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to coordinate the acquisition of input information for the estimating process for projects * Prepares project estimates using drawings, specifications, scope, basis of design, and other project information as input * Maintains files of project estimates and supporting backup documentation for reference during project execution * Supports the evaluation of historical data, including purchase orders, subcontracts, force reports, and productivity reports, and incorporates relevant data into the company unit cost, material, and labor factors * Monitors subcontract and purchase order awards, compares awarded costs to budgeted costs, determines the bases for variances, and adjusts estimating systems, models, and unit pricing to adjust future estimates * Reviews design options and recommends the best solutions based on cost, engineering quality, or availability of materials * Maintains relationships in the industry to facilitate the acquisition of estimating data and processes * Prepares estimates to support the change management process for projects * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to monitor project implementation for compliance with the basis of the project estimate as required * Evaluates required estimate contents by reviewing requests for proposal, attending pre\-bid meetings, and communicating with proposal team to support preparation of bids and proposals during bid/proposal preparation phase * Special projects as assigned Qualifications \& Requirements: * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline, or an equivalent technical degree. * Significant experience in Project Delivery, including Estimating and Cost. * 3\-6\+ years relevant industry experience. * Exposure to MEP and Cost Estimating systems is a distinct advantage. Skills* Written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to write clear reports to convey complex information simply to a diverse range of people. * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build and develop relationships. * Numerical and data analysis skills. * A creative and innovative approach to problem\-solving. * IT skills and the ability to learn sophisticated design and costing IT packages. * Teamwork skills and the ability to motivate and lead those on\-site. * Project management skills. * Resilience, determination, and the ability to work well under pressure. * Good commercial awareness. * A practical, logical, and methodical approach to work. * Attention to detail. * Detailed knowledge of past and current building and construction technology, processes, materials, business, and legal matters. All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required**. About Us: **IPS**, a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. IPS applies design and delivery expertise, client\-side experience, and supplier\-side insight to every project. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. That’s only possible when we care as much as our clients do. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV, and Staffing Services. *All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran, marital, or citizenship status, or any other status protected by law. description: **Join the Heart of the Kitchen as a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s!** **About Us:** At Carluccio’s, we believe that every great dish begins with a clean and organised kitchen. Our passion for authentic Italian cuisine is matched by our commitment to maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness and efficiency. We’re looking for a dedicated Kitchen Porter to join our team and keep our kitchen running smoothly. **The Role:** As a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s, you’ll play a crucial role in supporting our chefs and ensuring that the kitchen is always clean, safe, and well\-organised. Here’s what you’ll be doing: * Keep the Kitchen Spotless: Maintain cleanliness in the kitchen by washing dishes, utensils, and equipment, and ensuring work surfaces are clean and sanitary. * Support the Team: Assist chefs and kitchen staff with basic food preparation tasks, and help with organising and storing deliveries. * Manage Waste: Properly dispose of waste and recycling, keeping the kitchen area tidy and free of hazards. * Stock and Organize: Help with the organisation and restocking of ingredients and supplies, making sure the kitchen is always ready for service. * Ensure Safety: Follow all health and safety guidelines to ensure a safe working environment for everyone in the kitchen. What’s In It For You? We’re Not Just Offering a Job; We’re Offering a Career Full Of Excitement And Growth * Feast on 50% Off your total bill for you and 5 friends across all our brands—because great food should be shared! * Unlock Exclusive Discounts on thousands of online and high\-street retailers, plus restaurants, through our BRG Spark App. * Stock Up on Favourites: Enjoy 20% off at Carluccio’s retail gift shop \& deli. * Get Paid When You Need It with Wage Stream, offering access to advanced pay. * Refer Friends and Earn with our bonus scheme. * Skyrocket Your Career: Excellent opportunities for career development across our diverse brand portfolio. * Work Your Way: Enjoy flexible working opportunities that suit your lifestyle. This is your chance to be an essential part of our kitchen team, where every contribution is valued. If you’re ready to start your career in hospitality or take the next step, join us at Carluccio’s as a Kitchen Porter and become part of our family! **Boparan Restaurant Group (BRG)** is a growing hospitality group committed to our goal of striving to become the Best Restaurant Group in everything we do. We’re obsessed with building credibility, going above and beyond, and delivering a memorable guest experience and an amazing working environment. **At BRG, We Live By Our Core Values** ✅ **Honest** – Acting with integrity in everything we do. ✅ **Hardworking** – Giving our best, every day. ✅ **Hungry** – Always striving for growth and excellence. ✅ **Heart** – Caring deeply about our people, our guests, and our communities. Carluccio's on Dawson Street offers a relaxed, authentic Italian dining experience with a menu featuring classic dishes made from fresh, quality ingredients. **Skills** Fast\-Paced Experience

title: Knowledge Lawyer (Finance)
description: We are a leading international law and professional services firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to global corporations, financial institutions, capital market participants and investment fund managers. With a global presence spanning the Americas, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, we advise on the laws of Bermuda, the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Guernsey, Ireland and Jersey. We treat everyone as the intelligent professional they are. Our approach is to trust and empower our people to deliver consistently, and enable them to succeed. Diversity is our secret weapon – it’s the sheer breadth of Walkers people that makes us who we are – gathered from across the globe and fluent in languages, jurisdictions and cultures that help us to mirror our clients and keep our own thinking in tune with the world in which we operate. **The Role** Walkers is currently seeking to recruit an experienced and enthusiastic Knowledge Lawyer to join our highly regarded Finance and Capital Markets team in Dublin, on a permanent and full\-time basis (5 days). The option of a 4 day working week may be considered for the right candidate. Hybrid working options and flexible hours available. This is an exciting new role in our market\-leading and award\-winning structured finance \& securitisation practice. The successful candidate will work closely with the partners in the Irish office to help ensure that the team's knowledge and development objectives are achieved. We advise clients on the full range of banking, debt capital markets, finance, private credit, fund finance, real estate finance, securitisation and structured credit transactions. Your work will be interesting, rewarding, challenging and impactful. This is an excellent opportunity to work in an internationally focused, professional organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous personal professional development, mentoring and career development within a collegiate team\-based environment. The successful candidate will join our existing KM team and will support the strategies and processes to develop and maintain legal and professional resources including: * precedents; * know\-how; * client\-facing guides; * practice notes; * management of knowledge dissemination to Walkers lawyers; and * familiarity with Walkers external resources (for example library, journal subscriptions and online resources), In addition, they will also have the opportunity to support the Legal and Technical Training Manager in the development and delivery of training to the fee earners to enable them to effectively meet the needs of the clients in an evolving legal and regulatory landscape. This role offers the opportunity to drive excellence within the practice group while working closely with partners and senior knowledge lawyers to deliver high\-impact knowledge management and training for a high\-performing, dynamic and fast\-paced team. **Reporting** The role will report to the Co\-Heads of the FCM group. **Key Focus Areas** * Banking \& Finance * Debt Capital Markets * Derivatives \& risk transfer arrangements * Fund Finance * Green Finance and ESG products * Insurance\-Linked Securities * Private Credit * Real Estate Finance * Securitisation including ABS, CMBS, CLOs, RMBS \& synthetic securitisations * Securities law and regulatory considerations * Structured Credit * Structured Products * Company law **Duties \& Responsibilities** **Precedents and Know\-How** * Impact Assessments: monitoring and carrying out impact assessments on the implications of new legal and regulatory developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities for clients * Precedents \& Protocols: assisting with the maintenance and development of the Firm’s precedents, processes and knowledge systems, including automated documentation on DocumentDrafter, and providing related training and support where required * Current Awareness: management of timely knowledge dissemination to relevant fee earners * Horizon Scanning: reviewing existing, new and proposed legislation and case law, keeping abreast of developments in Irish \& EU legislation generally and disseminating the information thereon to relevant fee earners and other relevant practice groups **Training** * New Joiner Training: designing and maintaining suitable pre\-joining materials to be provided to new staff and assisting with the training of new fee earners in relation to the legal resources available, precedents, documentation standards \& protocols and other relevant knowledge materials * Legal Resources Training: train new lawyers, secretaries and corporate services executives on the availability of legal resources * Fee Earner Training: assisting with the coordination of a team\-wide training programme and assisting where possible with targeted training including the delivery of training sessions to fee earners on relevant aspects of Irish \& EU law and in relation to legal developments * Client Training: Preparing and delivering high\-quality briefing papers and presentations for clients on relevant legal and regulatory topics **Management and Administration** * Actively participating in the evaluation and implementation of legal technology solutions to improve knowledge management processes, internal protocols and to enhance research efficiency within the Firm * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with stakeholder practice groups and offices through a variety of channels including face\-to\-face meetings, regular group calls, attending wider practice group meetings, social events and incentive\-driven projects. * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with other business services departments (for example, preparing first drafts of thought leadership pieces/directory entries with the marketing and business development teams). * Support as directed the activities of the various Precedents and Know\-How Committees and/or the Global Opinion Committee, as applicable, as well as other working groups and committees by specific request. **Business Development** * Assisting in the production and/or updating of relevant Guides, thought leadership pieces, articles and practice notes * Liaising with senior fee earners and the Business Development Group in relation to website updates, thought leadership pieces, legal journal submissions etc **Skills \& Personal Attributes** The successful candidate should be a qualified solicitor or barrister in Ireland, Northern Ireland or England \& Wales with at least 4\-5 years PQE, ideally with a leading Irish or international law firm or in independent practice in the Law Library, or equivalent, with experience in one or more of finance, capital markets, structured finance \& securitisation, regulatory \& compliance, or general corporate. Candidate should have a proven strong academic track record including at least a 2:1 honours degree in any discipline. We are looking for candidates who can demonstrate the following: **Essential** * collegiate and thoughtful team player; * strong academic and experience credentials; * excellent research, analytical, technical, drafting skills and attention to detail; * an in\-depth knowledge of the types of resources for which a Knowledge Lawyer is responsible (for example, library resources, journals and online resources) and strong understanding of the importance of effective knowledge management within a law firm, with a particular focus on the needs of a busy Finance department; * client focused and commercial aptitude with an interest in/experience of business development and growing a service offering; * a calm and measured approach to problem solving; * excellent oral and written communication skills and ability to tailor this to communicate with partners, colleagues and (where appropriate) with the Firm's clients; * ability to work under pressure and responsive to the expectations of partners and clients; * able to prioritise competing demands; * excellent time management, project management and organisational skills; and * participate in continuously improving services through critically evaluating systems of work, promoting new ideas and actively developing service improvements. **Desirable:** * experience in Banking Asset Finance or Finance \& Capital Markets desirable, but not essential * experience with traditional online resources including BetterRegulation, WestLaw IE, Practical Law (PLC), Bloomsbury, Lexis\+, LMA online etc; * experience with Harvey AI, Co\-Pilot and other legal AI tech; * strong experience with Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and MS Excel. * knowledge of SharePoint, document management and document automation software an advantage. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Competitive Salary * Supportive, collegiate environment and open culture * Prominent international clients and challenging work * Working directly with partners * 'Dress for your day' dress code * Discretionary annual bonus scheme; * Generous annual leave * Agile Working Policy * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme and firm contribution * Life Assurance * Paid Maternity \& Paternity leave * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees paid * Learning \& Development and Mentorship programmes * Educational bursaries and assistance * Client secondment opportunities * Social Committee, DEI Committee, CSR Committee and various sports, social and charity initiatives * Subsidised gym access/fitness classes * Employee Assistance Programme * VDU eye testing and medical screening * Wellness programme Walkers global is an equal opportunity employer. Equality and diversity are key to our global identity and an integral part of our goal to continue being an employer of choice. We are committed to a work environment that supports all individuals irrespective of gender, ethnicity, nationality, race, religion, marital status, age, disability, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other applicable legally protected characteristics. We make every effort to ensure that employment opportunities are open and accessible to all purely on the basis of personal ability.
